notes: Confirmed: SKTouchEventArgs.WheelDelta property and SKTouchAction.WheelChanged enum exist in the shared API surface, but only Windows MAUI implements wheel event handling (via PointerWheelChanged + MouseWheelDelta). Apple, Android, and Tizen MAUI handlers hardcode WheelDelta=0 or use constructors that default it. Blazor, WPF, WinForms, and GTK4 views lack any touch/wheel handler infrastructure entirely. The reporter's claims about the current state are accurate.

reproductionSteps.description: Verify SKTouchEventArgs.WheelDelta property exists in shared API surface
reproductionSteps.output:
32: WheelDelta = wheelDelta;
50: public int WheelDelta { get; private set; }

reproductionSteps.description: Verify SKTouchAction.WheelChanged enum value exists in shared API
Confirm Windows MAUI handler implements wheel events with MouseWheelDelta (only working platform)
Confirm Apple MAUI handler has NO wheel/scroll handling — only UITouch events, WheelDelta defaults to 0
reproductionSteps.output: No matches (Apple handler only handles TouchesBegan/Moved/Ended/Cancelled via UIGestureRecognizer, uses basic constructor defaulting WheelDelta=0)

reproductionSteps.description: Confirm Android MAUI handler has NO wheel/AXIS_VSCROLL handling — hardcodes wheelDelta=0 in all event args
reproductionSteps.output: No matches (all SKTouchEventArgs constructors pass 0 for wheelDelta at lines 68, 83, 98, 104; no AXIS_VSCROLL or MotionEventActions.Scroll handling)

reproductionSteps.description: Confirm Tizen MAUI handler has NO wheel handling — uses basic constructor defaulting WheelDelta=0
reproductionSteps.output: No matches (Tizen handler only handles PointStateType Down/Up/Motion/Leave/Interrupted, uses 4-arg constructor that defaults WheelDelta=0)

reproductionSteps.description: Confirm Blazor WASM view has NO touch or wheel handler infrastructure
reproductionSteps.output: No matches (Blazor SKCanvasView only has PaintSurface event — no touch, mouse, or wheel event handling)

reproductionSteps.description: Confirm WPF and WinForms views have NO touch or wheel handler infrastructure
reproductionSteps.output: No matches in either file (WPF SKElement and WinForms SKControl only handle paint/render, no touch/wheel infrastructure)

reproductionSteps.description: Confirm GTK4 SKDrawingArea has NO scroll/wheel event controller
reproductionSteps.output: No matches (GTK4 SKDrawingArea only has PaintSurface event — no EventControllerScroll or wheel handling)

actionability.reason: Maintainer-filed tracking issue with 7 sub-issues (#3534-#3540). All claims verified — WheelDelta is only functional on Windows MAUI. No action needed on the parent issue; individual sub-issues track platform implementations.

actions.description: Add area/SkiaSharp.Views label since all changes are in view platform handlers

proposedResponse.body:
Thanks for the detailed tracking issue! We investigated the codebase and can confirm the current state as described:

**Investigation summary:**

| Platform | Wheel Support | Evidence |
|----------|--------------|----------|
| **Windows MAUI** | ✅ Working | `PointerWheelChanged` → `MouseWheelDelta` (v120 passthrough) |
| **Apple MAUI** | ❌ Missing | Only `UITouch` events; no scroll/wheel handling |
| **Android MAUI** | ❌ Missing | Only touch `MotionEvent`; no `AXIS_VSCROLL`; `wheelDelta` hardcoded to 0 |
| **Tizen MAUI** | ❌ Missing | Only `PointStateType` touch events; basic constructor defaults `WheelDelta=0` |
| **Blazor WASM** | ❌ Missing | No touch/wheel infrastructure in `SKCanvasView` |
| **WPF** | ❌ Missing | No touch handler infrastructure in `SKElement` |
| **WinForms** | ❌ Missing | No touch handler infrastructure in `SKControl` |
| **GTK4** | ❌ Missing | No `EventControllerScroll` in `SKDrawingArea` |

The shared API surface (`SKTouchEventArgs.WheelDelta` property + `SKTouchAction.WheelChanged` enum) already exists. Only the Windows MAUI handler wires it up. All other platforms either hardcode `WheelDelta=0` or lack touch/wheel handler infrastructure entirely.

The v120 standardization proposal and per-platform normalization formulas look well-defined. Sub-issues #3534–#3540 are ready to be worked independently.
